analysis_result: KNL1 (CASC5) mutation frequency in benign NF oncoprint: ~6.25% (1/16 Neurofibroma WES samples); in the combined benign+atypical NF cohort it is 8.3% (3/36) and in Atypical_NF alone it is 10.0% (2/20). The most likely frequency shown in the benign NF oncoprint of Supplementary Fig. 3a is approximately 6% (1/16 Neurofibroma samples).
denominator_used: N=16 (Neurofibroma WES samples). Alternatives: N=36 for combined Neurofibroma + Atypical_NF (benign/atypical NF WES cohort); N=20 for Atypical_NF only.
assumptions_made: KNL1 is equivalent to CASC5 (same gene, different Hugo symbol); 'benign NF' in Supplementary Fig. 3a is interpreted as the Neurofibroma-only cohort (atypical NFs typically shown separately); mutations with FILTER=PASS and TGL_FILTER_VERDICT=PASS are counted; sample mapping from clinical data 'WES label' to mutation 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' uses the prefix 'Tumor_'; 3 of 16 neurofibroma WES samples did not have matching mutation barcodes (may or may not have been included in the oncoprint).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 Clinical Data: 108 rows x 15 columns
   - MOESM5 Supplementary Table 2 (WES mutations): 7457 rows x 123 columns
   - Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label (clinical); Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification (mutations)

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter: WES == 'X' -> 54 samples
   - By Tumor Type: MPNST=15, Low-Grade MPNST=3, Atypical_NF=20, Neurofibroma=16
   - Benign NF cohort (Neurofibroma): N=16
   - Combined benign/atypical NF cohort: N=36

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - KNL1 also known as CASC5 (checked both gene symbols)
   - In MOESM5, only 'CASC5' symbol is used (3 rows total)
   - All three CASC5 mutations are Missense_Mutation with FILTER=PASS

D. COMPUTATION
   - Mapped WES samples to mutation barcodes via 'Tumor_' + WES label
   - CASC5 mutations found:
     * Tumor_1117 -> Atypical_NF
     * Tumor_1397 -> Neurofibroma
     * Tumor_1856 -> Atypical_NF
   - Neurofibroma only: 1/16 = 6.25%
   - Atypical_NF only: 2/20 = 10.00%
   - Combined (benign + atypical NF): 3/36 = 8.33%

E. RESULT
   - For 'benign NF oncoprint' (Neurofibroma only): 1/16 = 6.25% (~6%)
   - For combined benign/atypical NF cohort: 3/36 = 8.33% (~8%)

F. CAVEATS
   - 'Benign NF' can variably refer to neurofibroma alone or include atypical NF depending on context
   - In the paper's Supplementary Fig. 3a context, benign NF and atypical NF are typically displayed in separate oncoprints
   - 3 neurofibroma WES samples had no matching mutation data in MOESM5 (may indicate no mutations passed filters, or sample ID mismatch)
   - Without direct access to Supplementary Fig. 3a image, the exact displayed frequency cannot be confirmed
